entries.asBuilt: Money fields are whole-naira values across screens, mocks, and drift columns.
entries.targetState: Integer kobo (minor units) in the database and on the wire (Paystack-native). UI divides by 100 for display.
entries.reason: Float/whole-naira money is unsafe and mismatches Paystack + app-store billing reconciliation, which are minor-unit native.

entries.asBuilt: Drift uses int autoincrement primary keys.
entries.targetState: Client-generated UUIDv7 primary keys everywhere. Retrying a create with the same id is an idempotent no-op, not a duplicate.
entries.reason: Offline-first client must mint ids locally before sync; autoincrement cannot be reconciled across offline devices, and idempotent retries need a stable client id.

entries.asBuilt: Free-text / display-name plan literals were used in billing code (already fixed frontend-side in commit f476925, which now writes canonical ids).
entries.targetState: Canonical lowercase plan ids: starter | market | business | enterprise. Unknown values fail CLOSED to starter. Server is the sole entitlement authority.
entries.reason: Free-text plan strings are ambiguous and unsafe as an entitlement key; canonical ids are the authoritative entitlement literal.

entries.asBuilt: Staff screens render a granular per-staff permission matrix (record debts / record payments / send reminders / view reports / manage customers), held in-memory (kStaff/kStaffPerms), with no drift table.
entries.targetState: Coarse two-role model only: role = owner | staff. Staff can read/write debts and customers but cannot delete debts, manage staff, or touch billing/payout/subscription. No granular per-permission flags in the backend.
entries.reason: Owner decided the coarse model in handoff §5 (recorded decision > screen evidence). The granular matrix is not-yet-backed UI; the backend implements owner|staff.

entries.asBuilt: Reminder-tone third value is 'Firm' in setup_screen.dart _tones (~36-49), templates_screen.dart _tones (~13-26), and drift BusinessProfiles.reminderTone.
entries.targetState: Canonical reminder tones: gentle | friendly | final. The third tone is 'final' (not 'firm').
entries.reason: Product-truth T-27 (OweMe.pdf) names the third tone 'Final'; doc outranks as-built screen evidence. Resolved conflict C-1 by user ruling 2026-07-11.
